title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved classic in Hong Kong and Chinese bakeries, renowned for their distinctive crispy, sweet, crumbly topping that resembles a pineapple. Beneath this irresistible crust lies a soft, fluffy, slightly sweet bread roll, creating a delightful contrast of textures. These iconic buns offer a comforting taste of traditional Chinese baking, perfect for breakfast or a satisfying snack.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 250g all-purpose flour, 30g granulated sugar, 5g instant dry yeast, and 3g salt. Mix well. Add 120ml warm milk and 1 large egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Turn onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth. Incorporate 30g softened unsalted butter and knead for another 5-7 minutes until the dough is elastic and passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Proof the Dough
instructions.text: Place the kneaded dough in a lightly oiled bowl, turning once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size. This is your first proofing.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough is proofing, prepare the topping. In a medium bowl, cream 80g softened unsalted butter with 70g powdered sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in 1 large egg yolk and 1 tablespoon of evaporated milk until combined. Gradually add 150g all-purpose flour and mix until a soft, uniform dough forms. Do not overmix. Cover and chill in the refrigerator while the dough finishes proofing.

instructions.title: Shape Buns and Apply Topping
instructions.text: Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down and divide it into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Take the chilled topping dough, divide it into 8 equal portions, and flatten each portion into a thin disc (about 3-4 inches in diameter). Carefully drape a topping disc over each dough ball, gently pressing to adhere, leaving the bottom exposed. Use a knife to lightly score a crisscross or pineapple pattern on the topping.

instructions.title: Second Proof
instructions.text: Arrange the shaped buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving enough space between them. Cover loosely with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffed up. Preheat your oven to 190C (375F) towards the end of the second proofing.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together 1 large egg yolk and 1 teaspoon of water for the egg wash. Gently brush the top of each bun (the cookie topping part) with the egg wash. Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the buns are cooked through. Transfer to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

tags: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe
tips: Kneading the dough thoroughly is key for a soft, fluffy bun. Achieve the 'windowpane test' for best results, where a small piece of dough can be stretched thin enough to see light through without tearing.

For a perfectly crisp topping, ensure your butter is softened but not melted when making the cookie crust. Chilling the topping briefly can also make it easier to handle and prevent it from becoming too sticky.

Serve Pineapple Buns warm, fresh from the oven, for the ultimate experience. They are delicious on their own or traditionally enjoyed with a slice of cold butter inserted into the warm bun for a delightful sweet and savory contrast.
article:
Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved staple of Hong Kong bakeries and a true icon of Chinese baking. Despite their name, these delightful buns contain no pineapple; the name comes from the characteristic cracked, golden-brown topping that resembles the fruit's rough exterior. If you’ve ever dreamed of recreating that perfect blend of soft, fluffy bread and a sweet, crumbly, cookie-like crust in your own kitchen, you’re in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through mastering the authentic Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe, ensuring you achieve bakery-quality results every time.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

Pineapple Buns (Bo Lo Bao in Cantonese) are a sweet bread product popular in Hong Kong and Macau, as well as in Chinatowns worldwide. The magic of a good pineapple bun lies in its contrasting textures and flavors. The bread itself is incredibly soft, tender, and slightly sweet, often enriched with milk and butter. Capping this delightful base is the signature "pineapple crust" – a thin, sugary, and slightly crunchy topping that cracks beautifully during baking, giving it that distinctive appearance. This topping, made from a combination of flour, sugar, butter, and egg, is what truly sets Bo Lo Bao apart.

They are a quintessential comfort food, often enjoyed for breakfast, afternoon tea, or as a snack. The aroma of freshly baked Pineapple Buns wafting from a Chinese bakery is enough to entice anyone, and once you take a bite, you understand why they hold such a special place in the hearts of many. Learning the authentic Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe means bringing a piece of this culinary tradition into your home.

## Ingredients You Need

To embark on your journey of making the perfect Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao, you’ll need to gather ingredients for two main components: the soft bread dough and the iconic pineapple topping.

**For the Soft Bread Dough:**
*   **All-purpose flour or bread flour:** 300g (about 2 ½ cups) – Bread flour gives a chewier texture, while all-purpose is softer.
*   **Instant dry yeast:** 4g (about 1 ½ teaspoons)
*   **Granulated sugar:** 40g (about 3 tablespoons)
*   **Salt:** 3g (about ½ teaspoon)
*   **Warm milk:** 120ml (about ½ cup) – Around 100-110°F (38-43°C)
*   **Large egg:** 1, room temperature
*   **Unsalted butter:** 30g (about 2 tablespoons), softened

**For the Pineapple Topping:**
*   **Unsalted butter:** 60g (about ¼ cup), softened
*   **Powdered sugar:** 60g (about ½ cup)
*   **Large egg yolk:** 1, room temperature (reserve the white for egg wash)
*   **All-purpose flour:** 120g (about 1 cup)
*   **Baking powder (optional):** ½ teaspoon – For extra crispness
*   **Milk powder (optional):** 1 tablespoon – For richer flavor

**For the Egg Wash:**
*   **Reserved egg white (from topping):** 1
*   **Milk or water:** 1 tablespoon

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Creating the perfect Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao requires a few steps, but each one is manageable and rewarding. Follow this detailed Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe for success.

**1. Prepare the Bread Dough:**
*   In a large bowl, combine flour, yeast, sugar, and salt. Whisk well to distribute.
*   In a separate bowl, whisk together the warm milk and egg.
*   Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.
*   Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes until it becomes somewhat smooth.
*   Incorporate the softened butter gradually, kneading until the butter is fully absorbed and the dough is smooth, elastic, and passes the windowpane test (stretches thin enough to see light through without tearing). This may take another 8-10 minutes.
*   Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it proof in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size.

**2. Prepare the Pineapple Topping:**
*   While the dough proofs, prepare the topping. In a medium bowl, cream the softened butter and powdered sugar together until light and fluffy.
*   Beat in the egg yolk until well combined.
*   Sift in the flour (and baking powder/milk powder if using) and mix until a soft, pliable dough forms. Do not overmix.
*   Wrap the topping dough in plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes. This makes it easier to handle.

**3. Shape the Buns:**
*   Once the bread dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air.
*   Divide the dough into 8-10 equal portions (about 60-75g each). Roll each portion into a smooth ball.
*   Take the chilled topping dough and divide it into the same number of portions as your bread dough.
*   On a lightly floured surface or between two sheets of parchment paper, flatten one portion of the topping dough into a thin disc, large enough to cover about 2/3 to 3/4 of a bread dough ball.
*   Carefully place a topping disc over a bread dough ball. Gently press and smooth it around the top and sides, leaving a small portion of the bread exposed at the bottom. This allows the bread to rise without cracking the topping too much.
*   Using a sharp knife, gently score a criss-cross or spiral pattern on the topping to create the "pineapple" effect. Be careful not to cut through to the bread dough.
*   Place the shaped buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them.

**4. Second Proof and Bake:**
*   Cover the baking sheet loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let the buns proof again in a warm place for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.
*   Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C) about 15 minutes before the second proof is complete.
*   Whisk the reserved egg white with a tablespoon of milk or water for the egg wash.
*   Gently brush the egg wash over the scored topping of each bun.
*   Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and cracked, and the bread is cooked through.
*   Remove from the oven and transfer to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe outcome involves a few key techniques:

*   **Don't Rush Proofing:** Patience is crucial for light and airy bread. Ensure your dough doubles in size during the first proof and becomes visibly puffy during the second. A warm, draft-free environment is ideal.
*   **Proper Kneading:** For the bread dough, thorough kneading develops the gluten, which is essential for a soft, elastic texture. Don't be afraid to knead for the recommended time.
*   **Chill the Topping:** Chilling the pineapple topping dough makes it much easier to handle and prevents it from being too sticky when you're trying to flatten and apply it to the buns.
*   **Gentle Scoring:** When scoring the topping, use a light but firm hand. You want to create the pattern without cutting deeply into the bread dough underneath.
*   **Egg Wash for Shine:** The egg wash not only gives the topping a beautiful golden sheen but also helps to create that slightly crisp, caramelized surface.
*   **Even Baking:** Rotate your baking sheet halfway through baking if your oven has hot spots to ensure even browning.

## Serving Suggestions

The best way to enjoy a Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is fresh out of the oven, still warm. The contrast between the warm, soft bread and the slightly crisp, sweet topping is simply divine.

*   **The Classic Way:** In Hong Kong, it's common to slice the warm bun horizontally and insert a thick slab of cold butter, creating a "Bo Lo Yau" (pineapple bun with butter). The butter melts slightly into the warm bread, offering a rich, savory counterpoint to the sweetness.
*   **With a Beverage:** Pair it with a cup of traditional Hong Kong-style milk tea, coffee, or even a simple glass of cold milk for a comforting breakfast or afternoon snack.
*   **Plain and Simple:** Sometimes, the pure taste of a freshly baked pineapple bun is all you need. Enjoy it on its own, savoring every bite.
*   **With Spreads:** While not traditional, a light smear of jam or peanut butter can also be a delightful addition if you're looking for extra flavor.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe is perfect as is, there are always ways to experiment and adapt:

*   **Filled Pineapple Buns:** Many bakeries offer filled versions. Popular choices include:
    *   **Custard Cream:** A sweet, creamy egg custard filling.
    *   **Red Bean Paste:** A sweet, earthy paste made from adzuki beans.
    *   **Char Siu (BBQ Pork):** A savory and sweet BBQ pork filling, creating a unique sweet-and-savory experience.
    *   **Coconut Shreds:** A sweet coconut filling, often combined with butter and sugar.
*   **Topping Variations:**
    *   Add a pinch of almond flour to the topping for a slightly nutty flavor and texture.
    *   Sprinkle sesame seeds on the egg-washed topping before baking for added crunch and aroma.
*   **Dietary Adjustments:**
    *   **Vegan:** Substitute dairy milk with plant-based milk (almond, soy, oat), use vegan butter, and replace eggs with flax eggs or commercial egg replacers for both the dough and topping. Note that the texture will vary slightly.
    *   **Gluten-Free:** While challenging, you can experiment with gluten-free flour blends for the bread and topping. Look for blends designed for yeast baking.
*   **Simplifications:** If you're short on time, some home bakers use store-bought puff pastry for the topping, though it won't yield the exact same crumbly texture as the traditional pineapple topping.

Mastering the Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao Recipe is a rewarding culinary journey that brings the taste of a classic Hong Kong bakery right into your home. With patience and these detailed instructions, you’ll soon be enjoying your own warm, freshly baked Bo Lo Bao. Happy baking!
